items.title: City couple quit careers and take over outback pub

children.content: Burnt out from work and city life, Liz Murphy and Norm Matau knew they couldn't keep going. So they left it all behind to take over an iconic pub in a tiny outback town.

gallery.altText: A man and woman stand behind a bar in a pub.

thumbnail.altText: A man and woman stand behind a bar in a pub.

avatar.alt: A woman with a white blouse and glasses smiles as she looks towards the camera.

items.title: Victoria in the box seat after taking first-innings points in Shield final

children.content: Victoria is closing on the Sheffield Shield title after a dramatic day's play against South Australia, punctuated by confusion around an injury substitution.

image.altText: Mitch Perry runs off to celebrate a wicket with teammates while Nathan McSweeney walks off disappointed

items.title: A century ago, a Liberal Party disintegrated, and there are lessons for its namesake

children.content: Last week's South Australian election has left the Liberals in the grip of what one analyst described as an "existential crisis". As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

image.altText: A painting depicting a crowd in a street

items.title: Cotton and Davis involved in post-match spat after 36ers level NBL finals

children.content: Sydney coach Brian Goorjian insists he's not concerned by Kendric Davis's post-match tirade at Adelaide superstar Bryce Cotton, following the 36ers' NBL championship-levelling 91-89 win over the Kings.

image.altText: Tempers flare in the last quarter between Bryce Cotton of the Adelaide 36ers and Kendric Davis of the Sydney Kings

items.title: Palestinian-Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah speaks at NSW writers' festival

children.content: Her inclusion at the event was slammed by NSW Premier Chris Minns, but Dr Abdel-Fattah told an audience in Newcastle today that art and literature can be spaces for political expression.

image.altText: an author, holding a microphone, speaking to an audience

items.title: Liberals to retain SA opposition status despite One Nation's second seat, ABC projects

children.content: The ABC is projecting that the Liberals have retained the Adelaide Hills seat of Heysen, meaning the party will be the second largest in the House of Assembly and retain opposition status.

articles.title: Why isn't One Nation winning many seats in the SA election despite its high vote?

children.content: One Nation has received more than 20 per cent of the primary vote in South Australia, and so far, its lower house seat count remains slight. Why is that, and why are onlookers so interested in the result?

image.altText: A person voting on a sample House of Representatives ballot paper, with names like Apple and Peach

items.title: Bush artist 'Pro' Hart remembered 20 years after death

children.content: On March 28, 2006, Broken Hill lost one of its most renowned residents in internationally acclaimed artist Kevin "Pro" Hart. He was, and still is, considered a widely popular artist, but his recognition was divisive to different audiences.

image.altText: A man in a paint splattered blue shirt and glasses holding a paint brush and a paint board next to a painting of masked figures

items.title: Fans rethink driving to SA for AFL Gather Round as global fuel crisis continues

children.content: The SA government and AFL are in discussions to try to keep fans flocking to Adelaide for next month's AFL Gather Round, as the global fuel crisis threatens to disrupt travel plans.

image.altText: Multiple yellow signs reading 'pump not in use' attached to petrol pumps at a station

items.title: Hatchet attack on dog at SA golf course prompts public appeal

children.content: A kelpie that was found with "horrific" head wounds at an Adelaide Hills golf course has prompted the RSPCA to issue a public appeal for information.
Warning: This story contains details some readers may find distressing.

image.altText: A dog with stitches in its head.

items.title: Adrian Portelli's company found guilty of unlawful lotteries by SA court

children.content: Melbourne investor Adrian Portelli has been found not guilty of illegal lottery charges but his company has been ordered to pay tens of thousands of dollars in fines.

image.altText: A man holding 100 dollar bills in his hand

items.title: 'There is still hope': Former detective reflects six months on from Gus's disappearance

children.content: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

gallery.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

thumbnail.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

items.title: AFL admits crucial umpire error late in Crows' defeat to Cats

children.content: Geelong's Tom Atkins convinced umpires a free-kick was his in a decision the AFL has since clarified should have gone Adelaide's way in a tight contest.

image.altText: Tom Atkins and Zac Taylor contest a ball on the ground near their feet

items.title: Premier says regional SA will be first in line for fuel amid global crisis

children.content: South Australia's premier has assured farmers battling with soaring diesel prices that regional areas will be first to receive fuel deliveries.

image.altText: The silhouette of a man wearing a trucker cap standing next to a petrol bowser with a pump in hand.

items.title: 'Not acceptable': Xerri facing ban after rubbing blood on Dons skipper's face

children.content: North Melbourne survives a nervy finish to break a decade-long drought against Essendon as star ruckman Tristan Xerri rubs blood on Andrew McGrath's face in a heated exchange.

image.altText: Tristan Xerri rubs blood on the face of Andrew McGrath

items.title: In a quest for an affordable mortgage in our location of choice, my family tried apartment living

children.content: In a quest for an affordable mortgage in our location of choice, my family decide to rent an apartment as a trial. Will high-rise living be a realistic alternative to a house as a place to bring up our child?

image.altText: Inside the Southbank, Victoria, home of the McMurtie family.

items.title: Is Labor trusting big tech too much?

children.content: As an artificial intelligence revolution approaches, the government is responding to a huge opportunity in data-centre development while balancing copyright concerns and workforce anxiety.

image.altText: Rowland stands in the prime minister's courtyard smiling.

items.title: Cyclone Narelle destroys banana crops, fresh food growers hit hard

children.content: Fruit and vegetable producers in one of Western Australia's key food growing regions are counting the cost of Cyclone Narelle, with one banana grower reporting more than 80 per cent of his crop has been destroyed.

image.altText: A wide shot of a man wearing work uniform and walking through his banana plantation.

items.title: 'This cash cow is going to die': Why Chinese student numbers are falling

children.content: Chinese students continue to provide the lion's hare of revenue for Australian universities. But experts warn that could soon change.

gallery.altText: A group of students wearing graduation gowns hold flowers.

thumbnail.altText: A group of students wearing graduation gowns hold flowers.

items.title: Australia has to attack misinformation about climate change, Senate report says

children.content: Australia needs to increase its efforts to fight back against misinformation about climate change, a Senate report says.

image.altText: The silhouette of an oil pumpjack in the foreground with a wind turbine in the background.

items.title: Is this 'one stop shop' the way forward for Thriving Kids?

children.content: While serious workforce questions surround the new Thriving Kids scheme, the organisers of an existing program in Victoria believe its model could be scaled up.

image.altText: Two toddlers interacting in a playgroup

items.title: Obongjayar is the genre-defying Nigerian star challenging stereotypes

children.content: The Nigerian-British musician brightens up dark times, whether it's his adventurous solo music or scene-stealing collabs with Fred again.. and Little Simz.

gallery.altText: Nigerian-British man with short dreads, wearing black sleeveless top and puffy, fashionable pants, crouches with arms out front.

thumbnail.altText: Nigerian-British man with short dreads, wearing black sleeveless top and puffy, fashionable pants, crouches with arms out front.

items.title: Public transport to be free for Victorians in April

children.content: The Victorian government says public transport will be free across the state in April in a bid to relieve cost-of-living pressure as fuel prices continue to climb.

image.altText: Melbourne Metro city loop train

items.title: Consider yourself a trivia nut? Have a crack at this quiz

children.content: We know you've come here looking for trivia glory but you're unlikely to find any today.

image.altText: Tom Gleeson on the set of Hard Quiz gesturing behind him with a serious expression.

items.title: Farmers face 'decision paralysis' as fuel costs threaten viability

children.content: The sudden rise in fuel costs is forcing farmers to make difficult decisions, such as laying off workers and selling equipment to keep their businesses running.

items.title: Many Asians try to hide cultural clothing – now it's centre stage at an exhibition

children.content: For many South Asians, museums in the West have historically been places where their cultures were displayed as static artefacts. An exhibition like The Offbeat Sari has flipped that script, presenting South Asian creativity as innovative and globally influential.

image.altText: Priya Khanchandani wearing a cream coloured sari poses

items.title: WA businesses say matter of weeks before fuel crisis sparks serious price rises

children.content: Country business owners say they have worked to absorb price increases, but will be forced to pass on costs to consumers if the supply of fuel to regional WA isn't stabilised.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand holding a full pint of beer below a beer tap after pouring it at a bar in a pub.

items.title: From confectionery to boot polish, show bags were originally free sample bags

children.content: The "uniquely Australian" tradition of the show bag dates back more than a century, and shows no sign of dying out.
